title: Starting Your Studies
text: Starting Your Studies: HAW-Hamburg: Starting your studies Welcome first-semester students! We want to help make the start of your studies as pleasant and easy as possible. We've compiled the most important information here. The HAW Hamburg Student Center wishes you success in your studies! Download the checklist for starting your studies here. As early as possible - start these around 10 weeks before semester begins Get ready to register (enrol) with us ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Check what documents you will need to submit for registering as a student at HAW Hamburg. Good to know : You’ll need to submit some documents by post as hard copies. Make sure you keep to the deadline for submission – it’s very soon after you receive your confirmation of admission. Check and secure your student finance ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Find out as early as you can about funding options, such as BAföG and opportunities for scholarships . If you want to find a part-time student job , you should start applying now. Check to see if the timetable for your degree course is online, so you can factor it in to your plans. Good to know : If you are eligible for BAföG, you will start receiving it for each month from the month you submit your application, so make sure you have done this by the month in which you start your degree. Find somewhere to live ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If you don’t have a place to live yet, now’s the time to start looking for an apartment or a room in a shared flat, or to apply for a room in a hall of residence ( Studierendenwohnheim ). Try a housing association ( Wohnungsbaugenossenschaft ) – they can be a good source of a home for a student budget. Good to know : There’s a shortage of affordable accommodation in Hamburg. Make sure you give yourself enough time to find something! The main phase - get going on these tasks around 8 weeks before the start of semester Start the registration (enrolment) process ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Complete your registration documents and make sure you submit them before the deadline. You’ll need to pay your semester contribution by the deadline. Good to know : Ensure you submit all documents required, properly completed, to avoid delays to the registration process viaMint - Get planning your first semester ................................................................................................................................................................................................ You can refresh and advance your maths, physics, chemistry and/or computer science skills using the viaMINT online learning environment . Once you’ve registered for your degree course, you can log in to viaMINT using your HAW Hamburg account. How does viaMINT work? Log in on viamint.de (in German only), using either your personal or HAW Hamburg email address, and select your degree course in the " Auswahl einer individuellen Lernoberfläche " dialogue window. You’ll be shown modules with content that is relevant to your course and can work through them. Preliminary courses - Get planning your first semester ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Some degrees run one- or two-week preliminary courses in subjects such as maths before lectures begin. Why not

title: Starting Your Studies
text: Starting Your Studies: check if your degree course is one of them? The courses can help you get a great start to your studies. Orientation events - Get planning your first semester ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Make room in your schedule for your welcome and orientation events . They’re important for finding out about your timetable, any groups you may be working in, and who to turn to if you need advice or help. They also represent an opportunity to get to know the campus and the other students on your course. Activate your HAW Hamburg account ................................................................................................................................................................................................ It’s time to activate your HAW Hamburg online account and set up 2-factor authentication. You’ll need your account for all HAW Hamburg’s online services, such as software and our learning gateway. Please note : You should use your @haw-hamburg.de email address (in Outlook) to receive official information from HAW Hamburg. You can set up forwarding of all messages to your personal email – this will make sure you don’t miss anything important. Good to know I: You will soon find everything you need on myHAW - e.g. your student ID, BAföG confirmation, grades and much more. Before you start: Activate your HAW Hamburg account. Then log in to myHAW via the blue ‘SSO access’ tile (no longer with the applicant login at the top right!). Good to know II : HAW Hamburg provides its students with various tech facilities and resources, including use of Microsoft 365, CoPilot and anti-virus software, wifi, and e-books. Have a look and make use of our resources to help you in your studies. Get your tech ready ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Make sure that you have an internet-enabled device. At the latest, you’ll find out exactly what tech you need for your degree course in the first few weeks of lectures. You should also ensure that you have access to HAW Hamburg’s online services and resources , including myHAW, Moodle etc. Good to know : Eduroam wifi is free of charge and available across all our premises and facilities, and you can also use it at other higher education institutions. You can set it up using the Easyroam app, once you have activated your HAW Hamburg account/Microsoft 365 and set up the SSO login process. The final countdown - you need to start these tasks around 4 weeks before semester kicks off Download your student ID and confirmation of Bafög funding ................................................................................................................................................................................................ You should now download your student ID and confirmation of Bafög funding – you can do this easily on myHAW , HAW Hamburg’s student services gateway. Find support for your particular needs ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Do you have a disability or a long-term health condition ? We’re here to support you with any challenges you face. Contact HAW Hamburg’s advice services with your questions and support needs, and we’ll do our best to help. HAW Hamburg’s Family Office can provide support if you need to balance your studies with caring for a child/children or a family member . Work out what’s where on campus and in

title: Starting Your Studies
text: Starting Your Studies: the area nearby ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If you are able to move to or visit Hamburg before semester starts, take a look at your campus and find out where all the key places are (the library, cafeteria, lecture halls/teaching rooms, etc.). Work out how to get from your accommodation to campus – will you cycle or use public transport, for example? Download your semester ticket ................................................................................................................................................................................................ HAW Hamburg uses the Deutschland Ticket as your semester ticket . You can travel on it from the official beginning of semester – that’s 1 April or 1 October. Ready to launch - you should do these things around the time classes begin Start getting to know the campus, your degree course, and other students ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Take part in orientation weeks , welcome events and campus tours for your degree course or your campus – they’ll help you settle in. Good to know I : Try and chat to other students so you can build up a social circle. Good to know II : From now on, you’ll be getting all the vital information from us via your @haw-hamburg.de email address – so make sure you check it regularly! Good to know III : Our new students’ support team runs classes for first-semester students delivered by peer tutors – they’re great for helping you find your feet in your course. Welcome on Instagram ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Why not follow us on Instagram as we welcome all the new students? We’ve got lots of important info and tips for you as you launch yourself into your studies. Not there but still informed ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If you couldn’t be in Hamburg in the opening week, make sure you find out key information from other students or contact someone from your course who can help – peer tutors, your academic advisor or course coordinator. Apply for your library card ................................................................................................................................................................................................ You can apply for your library card online – it’s easy. Once you’ve done that, you can collect it when the HAW Hamburg libraries are open and find out how to borrow books, journals etc. in physical and electronic format. Do your safety training ................................................................................................................................................................................................ HAW Hamburg requires all students to complete mandatory online safety training , which gives you important information on action to take in emergencies and other health and safety-related issues. It’s available on Moodle and you complete it on your own. Any more questions? Your direct contact ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If you have any questions, please get in touch with us using the contact form ! title: Starting Studies at Faculty EMI
text: Starting Studies at Faculty EMI: HAW-Hamburg: Starting your Studies at the Faculty EMI Electrical Engineering, Media and Information Technology Study and teaching Starting your Studies at the Faculty EMI (current) Course timetables Options for starting your Master's studies Academic Course Advisors Exams Academic Office Dear students, welcome to the Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Media and Information Technology at HAW Hamburg! Orientation Unit for the degree program Media Technology The summer semester starts for first-year students on Tuesday, April 7, 2026, with the orientation unit. During the first week of classes, the so-called orientation unit ("Orientierungseinheit/OE") will be held for first-year students of the bachelor’s degree program Media Technology. Attendance at this unit is mandatory. We will begin on Tuesday, April 7, 2026, at 9:30 a.m. at the production lab ("Produktionslabor") at the Hamburg Art and Media Campus (Building C (Tide Building), Finkenau 35, 22081 Hamburg). Please arrive in time for the start of the event, but no earlier than 15 minutes beforehand. This is a series of various events designed to help first-year students adjust to life at the HAW and introduce them to the challenges of their studies and academic life. Another key objective is to break down the barriers of anonymity among students as early as possible—ideally within the first week—through personal interactions in small work and discussion groups, as well as through shared leisure activities. Specifically, courses on the following topics are offered—these may vary from semester to semester: Getting to know your classmates, older students, and professors Exploring the buildings, libraries, laboratories, and cafeteria Organization and self-management of your study program Information about the Student Council and the General Student Committee Methods and challenges of group work Information about career opportunities Getting to know Hamburg as a place to study Orientation Units for the degree programs Electrical Engineering and Information Technology and Renewable Energy Systems and Energy Management An orientation unit ("Orientierungseinheit/OE") is held at the start of the program. During this unit, you will receive all the important information you need to start your studies, you will be assigned to your semester groups, and complete your registration on the computer network—therefore, attending is essential for a smooth start into your first semester. The orientation unit will take place from April 7, 2026, to April 10, 2026. For more information about the orientation unit, please visit the website of the orientation unit . Preliminary Courses for the degree programs Electrical Engineering and Information Technology and Renewable Energy Systems and Energy Management Mathematics is essential for success in a degree program in Electrical Engineering, Media, and Information Technology. The degree programs at the Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Media, and Information Technology therefore require a certain level of foundational knowledge in mathematics. To refresh and reinforce this foundational knowledge, we offer first-year students a mathematics preparatory course before each semester, which combines supervised sessions with self-study units on the online learning platform viaMINT—video-based interactive preparatory courses. The mathematics preparatory course is offered exclusively for first-year students of the following bachelor’s degree programs: Electrical

title: Starting Studies at Faculty EMI
text: Starting Studies at Faculty EMI: Engineering and Information Technology Information Engineering Media Technology und Renewable Energy Systems and Energy Management The preparatory course will take place from March 18 to April 2, 2026. On March 18, 2026, the preparatory course begins at 8:30 a.m. and ends at 1:00 p.m. On all other days, the preparatory course begins at 9:00 a.m. and ends at 1:00 p.m. on March 18, 2026, the introductory course will take place at the Berliner Tor 7 building in room 13.01. On the other days, the course will take place in room 4.05 (also Berliner Tor 7). We recommend the in-person preparatory course. If you are unable to attend in person, we also offer an online preparatory course via Zoom. Registration is not required. In-person preparatory course Welcome session, placement test, first class Course content: Term transformation Fractions Powers and roots Logarithms Equations and inequalities Systems of equations Functions Trigonometry Start of the semester for the master’s programs Automation Technology, Information and Communication Engineering, and Microelectronic Systems In these master’s programs, a welcome session and the selection of elective courses take place on the first day of classes. All students admitted to the master’s programs will be contacted individually shortly before the start of the semester and will receive all the necessary information regarding the location, room, and current course options. During the welcome session, you can also ask any questions you may have about the program. (These dates will be updated further) Webteam Faculty of Electrical Engineering, Media and Information Technology / last change 18.03.2026 x

title: Semester Ticket / Germany Ticket
text: Semester Ticket / Germany Ticket: HAW-Hamburg: Semesterticket Studien­orga­ni­sation Studien­gänge Semesterzeiten Vorlesungsverzeichnis und Stundenplan Rückmeldungen, Änderung von Daten Chipkarte Semesterticket (current) Prüfungen & Zeugnisse Beurlaubung und Exmatrikulation Ordnungen Seit dem Sommersemester 2024 können Sie Ihr Semesterticket / Deutschlandticket über das Campus-Portal RIDE des HVV herunterladen und im Wallet Ihres Smartphones speichern. Voraussetzung dafür ist, dass Sie sich mit der Zahlung des Semesterbeitrags ordnungsgemäß zurückgemeldet haben, oder sich, wenn Sie neu an der HAW Hamburg sind, bereits eingeschrieben haben. Sie wird gesetzlich vertreten durch Prof. Dr. Ute Lohrentz, Präsidentin der HAW Hamburg, Berliner Tor 5, 20099 Hamburg. 2. Die Verarbeitung der personenbezogenen Daten im Zusammenhang mit dem Semesterticket/Deutschlandticket ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Zur Nutzung des Angebots benötigt die S-Bahn Hamburg GmbH einen Nachweis, dass die jeweiligen Studierenden zum Erwerb des Deutschlandtickets berechtigt sind. Diese Überprüfung Ihrer personenbezogenen Daten wird an der HAW Hamburg mithilfe der Übermittlung einer CSV-Datei an einen von der S-Bahn Hamburg GmbH, bzw. deren Dienstleister Digital H bereitgestellten SFTP-Server durchgeführt. 3.1 Datenkategorien Mittels CSV-Datei werden folgende Daten an die S-Bahn Hamburg GmbH übermittelt: Interne ID Matrikelnummer Nachname, Vorname Geburtsdatum Current Semester From / To Next Semester From / TO 3.2 Rechtsgrundlage Die Verarbeitung der personenbezogenen Daten erfolgt aufgrund einer Vertragserfüllung, Art. 6 Abs. 1 lit. b DSGVO. 3.3 Speicherdauer Die Daten werden an die S-Bahn Hamburg GmbH, bzw. deren Dienstleister Digital H übermittelt. Weitere Informationen zur Löschung bei dem Vertragspartner sowie dem Auftragsverarbeiter können Sie den jeweiligen Datenschutzinformationen entnehmen. 3.4 Weiterleitung der Daten Die HAW Hamburg tritt für die Studierendenschaft, vertreten durch den AStA, als Datenmittler an die S-Bahn Hamburg GmbH auf und stellt hierfür eine CSV-Datei zur Verfügung. 4. Ihre Rechte ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Sie haben das Recht auf eine Auskunft über die von Ihnen gespeicherten personenbezogenen Daten sowie das Recht auf Berichtigung, Löschung oder auf Einschränkung der Verarbeitung der von uns erhobenen Daten. Sie haben das Recht, aus Gründen, die sich aus ihrer besonderen Situation ergeben, jederzeit gegen die Verarbeitung der Sie betreffenden personenbezogenen Daten, die aufgrund von Art. 6 Abs. 1 lit. e oder f DSGVO erfolgt, Widerspruch einzulegen. Sie haben das Recht, Ihre datenschutzrechtliche Einwilligungserklärung jederzeit zu widerrufen. Durch den Widerruf der Einwilligung wird die Rechtmäßigkeit der aufgrund der Einwilligung bis zum

title: Semester Ticket / Germany Ticket
text: Semester Ticket / Germany Ticket: Widerruf erfolgten Verarbeitung nicht berührt. Unbeschadet eines anderweitigen verwaltungsrechtlichen oder gerichtlichen Rechtsbehelfs steht Ihnen das Recht auf Beschwerde bei einer Aufsichtsbehörde zu, wenn Sie der Ansicht sind, dass die Verarbeitung der Sie betreffenden personenbezogenen Daten gegen die DSGVO verstößt. Weitere Infor­mationen Kontakte bei Fragen und Problemen ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Die wichtigsten Fragen und Antworten sowie weitere Informationen findet sich hier: hvv.de/studierende Bei weiteren Fragen wenden Sie sich an SBHH.Kundensupport (at) deutschebahn (dot) com oder per Telefon an 040 3918 3900. Wenn Sie eingeschrieben sind und den Semesterbeitrag bezahlt haben und sich trotzdem auch am Tag nach der offiziell erfolgenden Rückmeldung / Einschreibung nicht einloggen können, schreiben Sie eine Mail an: myHAW (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de Ich kann oder will kein mobiles Endgerät für das Ticket nutzen. Was kann ich tun? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Bitte wenden Sie sich diesbezüglich an den AStA. Weitere Informationen finden Sie auf der Seite des AStA . Befreiung vom Semesterticketbeitrag / Rückerstattung ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Sie können sich vom Semesterticketbeitrag befreien lassen, wenn einer der folgenden Gründe auf Sie zutrifft: Schwerbehinderung Beurlaubung Auslandsaufenthalt Immatrikulation und Ticketbezug an anderer Hochschule (z. B. im Falle eines Doppelstudiums an zwei Hochschulen) Die Liste der Befreiungsgründe ist abschließend, d.h. wenn Sie keinen der genannten Gründe haben, ist ein Antrag aussichtslos. Bitte prüfen Sie in dem Fall, ob für Sie eine Erstattung über den Semesterticket-Härtefonds in Frage kommt. Den Antrag auf Befreiung stellen Sie bitte per E-Mail an sts (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de . Für den Antrag sind folgende Nachweise erforderlich: Schwerbehinderung: Schwerbehindertenausweis mit Gültigkeit und Beiblatt zum Schwerbehindertenausweis inklusive zugehöriger Wertmarke Beurlaubung: Der Antrag auf Beurlaubung muss bewilligt sein, damit die Befreiung bewilligt wird – bitte stellen Sie daher den Antrag auf Beurlaubung vor dem Antrag auf Befreiung. Auslandsaufenthalt: Nachweis über einen studiumsbezogenen Auslandsaufenthalt - z.B. Zulassungsbescheid oder Immatrikulationsnachweis oder entsprechende Bescheinigung der ausländischen Hochschule, Nachweis über ein studiumsbezogenes Auslandspraktikum o.ä. Ticketbezug an anderer Hochschule: Nachweis der Immatrikulation an einer anderen deutschen Hochschule und Bescheinigung der Hochschule darüber, dass dort ein Ticket abgenommen wird. Maximal beantragbare Dauer der Befreiung: Schwerbehinderung: für die Dauer der Gültigkeit des Wertmarke Beurlaubung: für die Dauer der Bewilligung der Beurlaubung (bei einer Folgebeurlaubung muss der Antrag auf Erstattung erneut gestellt werden!) Auslandsaufenthalt: für die Dauer des Aufenthalts laut Bescheinigung (semesterweise) Ticketbezug an anderer Hochschule: 1 Semester Antragsfristen: Bis zu vier Wochen nach Vorlesungsbeginn Ausnahmeregelungen/Verlängerungen der Fristen sind aus vertraglichen Gründen nicht möglich. Wird Ihr Antrag bewilligt, werden Sie für die Dauer der Bewilligung von der Zahlung des Semesterbeitragsanteils für das Ticket befreit. Erfolgt die Bewilligung vor der Zahlung des Semesterbeitrags für das bzw. die betroffenen Semester, wird der Anteil von Ihrer Forderung abgezogen und Sie erhalten einen neuen Beitragsbescheid/Musterzahlträger mit dem reduzierten Betrag. Erfolgt die Bewilligung nach der Zahlung des Semesterbeitrags, wird Ihnen der Anteil für das Ticket auf das im Antrag angegebene Konto erstattet. Bitte beachten Sie: Sobald Ihr Antrag auf eine Befreiung vom Semesterticket von Ihnen abgeschickt und bewilligt wurde, kann die gewährte Befreiung bis zum Ende des laufenden Semesters, in dem die Befreiung beantragt wurde, nicht mehr rückgängig

title: Digital Infrastructure & Services
text: Digital Infrastructure & Services: HAW-Hamburg: Digital infrastructure and services Digitalisation Our strategy What we do Digital infrastructure and services (current) A modern and powerful IT infrastructure and the accompanying support are requirements for teaching, learning, study, research and administrative work. Through the ongoing development of its internal processes, digital infrastructure and IT services, HAW Hamburg is adapting its IT landscape to meet the demands and needs in the areas of studies, teaching, research and continuing development. In the area of digital infrastructure and IT services, HAW Hamburg is pursuing the following goals: 1. Reorganisation and further development of the IT Service Center (ITSC) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The IT Service Centre (ITSC) is being reorganised and further developed – in terms of staff, organisation and technology. The demands on the centrally provided IT services have increased significantly and the effective implementation of the digitalisation strategy is only possible with these measures. 2. IT governance ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Well-founded and strategic decisions in the IT area are only possible when decision-making responsibilities are clearly and efficiently defined. For this reason, HAW Hamburg is optimising its IT governance. 3. IT structures ................................................................................................................................................................................................ In order to respond to university members' changing needs and demands, the technical and organisational IT structures and processes across the entire university are being adapted to current requirements in the areas of teaching, learning, studies, research, administration and continuing education. 4. Information security management ................................................................................................................................................................................................ In order to systematise and document the diverse security measures and policies, HAW Hamburg aims to develop an information security management system. title: ITSC – IT Service Center
text: ITSC – IT Service Center: HAW-Hamburg: Informationstechnik Service Center (ITSC) Das Informationstechnik Service Center (ITSC) ist der zentrale IT‑Dienstleister der HAW Hamburg. Wir sorgen für den zuverlässigen Betrieb, die Weiterentwicklung und die Sicherheit der zentralen IT‑Infrastruktur – damit Lehre, Forschung, Verwaltung und Services reibungslos funktionieren. Unser Fokus liegt auf stabilen IT‑Diensten, klaren Zuständigkeiten und praxisnaher Unterstützung für alle Beschäftigten der HAW Hamburg. IT‑Support & UserHelpDesk Der UserHelpDesk (UHD) ist die zentrale Kontaktstelle für IT‑Anliegen der Beschäftigten. Hier werden Störungen aufgenommen, Anfragen koordiniert und Lösungen bereitgestellt – telefonisch, per E‑Mail oder über Ticketsysteme. Accounts, Identitäten & Zugänge Wir betreiben die zentrale Benutzer‑ und Rechteverwaltung der HAW Hamburg, darunter: HAW‑Account / Microsoft 365 Single‑Sign‑on (SSO) Zwei‑Faktor‑Authentifizierung (MFA) VPN‑Zugänge Applikationen & Systeme Das ITSC verantwortet Betrieb und Weiterentwicklung zentraler Anwendungen, u. a.: myHAW und Campus‑Management‑Systeme Zentrales Content‑Management‑System (TYPO3) Fachanwendungen für Verwaltung, Lehre und Organisation Netzwerk & Kommunikation Wir planen, betreiben und sichern die Kommunikationsinfrastruktur der HAW Hamburg: Datennetz und WLAN (inkl. eduroam) Anbindung an das Wissenschaftsnetz (DFN) Telefonie‑ und Kommunikationslösungen Infrastruktur & Betrieb Zum Aufgabenbereich gehören: Server‑, Storage‑ und Virtualisierungsplattformen Betrieb zentraler Rechenzentrumsdienste Sicherstellung von Verfügbarkeit, Performance und Backup Lizenzen, Verträge & Vergaben Das ITSC koordiniert zentrale IT‑Rahmenverträge und unterstützt bei: Software‑Lizenzmanagement (z. B. Microsoft, Adobe, SPSS) IT‑Vertragsmanagement IT‑Beschaffung und Vergabeverfahren (EVB‑IT) Online-Services myHAW, HAW-Mail, WLAN/eudroam, VPN, Single-Sign-On-Services, etc. Weitere Informationen im Beschäftigtenportal itsc-support (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de Telefon UHD: +49.40.21904-11880 Mo - Mi 08:00 - 16:00 Uhr Do 10:00 - 16:00 Uhr Fr 08:00 - 14:00 Uhr Hinweis: Aus Sicherheitsgründen werden KEINE Passwörter oder die 2-Faktor-Authentifizierung am Telefon zurückgesetzt! title: Libraries Overview
text: Libraries Overview: HAW-Hamburg: Libraries Libraries Research resources Using the libraries Opening hours and locations Publishing Services HAW catalogue Publishing Library card Opening hours and locations Focus, learn and understand: You can find what you're looking for in the HAW Hamburg libraries. The five HAW Hamburg libraries are home to over 200,000 print publications. We also have a large selection of e-books and e-journals. In addition to study rooms and Internet workspaces, we also provide expert advice and assistance. E-media resources available through the HAW libraries ................................................................................................................................................................................................ HAW Hamburg members – employees, students and instructors – can access numerous online resources from the HAW network. This access is available at the university via WLAN or when you are not physically at the university from outside via VPN or Shibboleth. 1. VPN connection Almost all of the e-media licensed for use at HAW Hamburg can also be accessed remotely using the VPN connection. You can find more information about using the VPN connection here . Once you have connected to the HAW network using the VPN connection, you can search the HAW catalogue as you normally would and read full-text versions of online resources. For questions related to our e-media resources, please contact your library using this form . 2. Shibboleth access Shibboleth provides another way of accessing electronic resources. You can use your HAW account to log in, either via the Shibboleth portal at https://login.haw-hamburg.de or directly from your search in the HAW catalogue . A VPN connection is not necessary. Loans, borrowing (book returns) and on-site scans ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The HAW Hamburg libraries are open during the periods listed on the website . You can work in the libraries and use the print materials on site. You can also scan print materials in all of the HAW libraries. Borrowing of library items is possible in all of the HAW Hamburg libraries: at the self-service checkout stations at all times during opening hours, and at the service desks when these are open. Returns via the automated returns shelves are possible at all times during opening hours . Reserving and ordering library items ................................................................................................................................................................................................ You can use the HAW Catalogue to order items from other HAW libraries for pick up in your HAW library or to reserve items that are currently on loan . Both services are free of charge and are available to all library users. More information about reserving and ordering library items Library workspaces for studying ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Individual and group workspaces are available in all HAW libraries. The group workspaces in Campus Library Finkenau (Finkenau 35), Campus Library Bergedorf (Ulmenliet 20), Campus Library Alexanderstraße (Alexanderstraße 1) and HIBS Learning Center (Berliner Tor 5) can be booked online. This service is only available to members of HAW Hamburg. It is no longer possible to book the group workspaces at the counter or on paper. The terms of use, access to registration and further information can be found under Reserve group workspaces . Contact information ................................................................................................................................................................................................ For general requests and questions, please use our

You can also write directly to HIBS_Service (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de . title: Library Opening Hours and Locations
text: Library Opening Hours and Locations: open to students. During these opening hours you can work, read and do research in our libraries. It is also possible to borrow and return items. Borrowing Borrowing library items is possible via the self-service checkout stations. Automated returns All of the HAW Hamburg libraries have automated returns shelves, which automatically record the returns when books are placed in them. Most items are then immediately available for borrowing again. Returns bins It is possible to return items via the returns bins – even when the libraries are closed: Returns bin, Ulmenliet 20, left of the Mensa beside the blackboard and the cement pillars Returns bin, Finkenau 35, new building Returns bin, Alexanderstrasse 1, in the foyer of Haus A Please note that the return is not recorded in the system until the next day the library is open with service desk . This date is considered the return date. Immediate borrowing of the item or the payment of fees is not possible. Social Media Social Media We have an account on Mastodon ! University Information and Library Service (HIBS) / last change 08.04.2026 x

title: Using the Libraries
text: Using the Libraries: HAW-Hamburg: Using the libraries Libraries Using the libraries Library card Your user account Fees Opening hours and locations Logging in to your user account Library card Information for students with a disability or chronic illness Haven't found what you are looking for or want to make a purchase suggestion? You can find more information about VPN, Shibboleth, research and more on the Services page. User account How do I access my user account? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ When you use the HAW catalogue , you can view your borrowing account via your user account (under 'Account login' at the top-right side of the screen). Your account number is printed under the barcode on your library card. If you use your chip card as your library card, your user number is printed on the back of the card. The initial password is always the first three letters of your last name (lower case). You can find more information on the Your user account page. What do I do if I've forgotten my password? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ To reset your password, click on the 'Account login' button at the top right of the HAW catalogue page, then click on 'Forgot password' in the login window. How do I extend my loan of a book from the HAW catalogue? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The loan period for a book from the university libraries is four weeks. It is then possible to extend your loan three times, each time for a period of four weeks. The new return date will be based on the date of the extension request. Students with a disability or a chronic illness can be granted longer loan periods to compensate for disadvantages. You can find more information in this leaflet: Information for students with a disability or chronic illness It is not possible to extend the loan of a library item if another library user has reserved the book, the loan period has already expired, or the loan period has already been extended three times. In these cases you are required to return the book. You can do this in each of the HAW libraries , regardless of where you borrowed the book. In all other cases you can extend your loan online via the HAW catalogue . Borrowing from the libraries How do I borrow an item? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Please take books you wish to borrow from the shelf yourself and check them out using the self-service checkout machine . To borrow books, you will need a library card or an activated HAW Hamburg chip card. More information about the library card Are there certain types of publications that can't be borrowed? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Many journals and a number of books are part of the reference collection and are unfortunately not available for loan. Items that are reserved for a particular seminar and are therefore part of the professor's semester syllabus can also usually not be borrowed. However, it is generally possible to borrow these items overnight or over the weekend. If you have additional questions, please contact

title: Using the Libraries
text: Using the Libraries: the loans desk of the library in question. Can library items be picked up by someone other than me? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Yes. If you are unable to pick up library items in person, please give the person who will be picking them up your library card or your chip card (activated for use as a library card) and a completed and signed form authorising them to pick up library items (signed no more than seven days previously). What is the maximum number of books that can be borrowed? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Lecturers at HAW Hamburg may borrow up to 100 books; for lecturers at other universities, students at HAW Hamburg and other state universities in Hamburg, and staff at HAW Hamburg, the limit is 50. HAW Hamburg alumni may borrow up to 20 books, whilst all other users may borrow a maximum of 10 books. How long can I borrow library items for? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ When you borrow a book from the university libraries, the intial loan period is four weeks (28 days). Students with a disability or a chronic illness can be granted longer loan periods to compensate for disadvantages. You can find more information in this leaflet: Information for students with a disability or chronic illness You can view the due dates for items you have borrowed in your user account. You will usually receive an automatically generated reminder of the due date three days before the library items are due. We must have your current email address on file for this to take place. If you have missed the due date, you will receive an overdue notice by email. Overdue fees will have already been charged at this point. These automatically generated emails are a service of the University Information and Library Service. Users are still responsible for returning borrowed items on time. If you do not return borrowed items by the due date you will be charged an overdue fee. You can find more information on the Fees page. Will I receive a reminder email about items that are due to be returned? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ You will usually receive an automatically generated reminder of the due date three days before the library items are due. We must have your current email address on file for this to take place. If you have missed the due date, you will receive an overdue notice by email. Overdue fees will have already been charged at this point. These automatically generated emails are a service of the University Information and Library Service. Users are still responsible for returning borrowed items on time. How do I extend the borrowing period or return an item? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The loan period for a book can usually be extended three times. Please note that the new return date will be based on the date of the extension request. You can extend your loan online via the HAW catalogue . It is not possible to extend the loan period for a library item if another library user has reserved the book,

title: Using the Libraries
text: Using the Libraries: the loan period has already expired, or the loan period has already been extended three times. In these cases you are required to return the book. You can do this in each of the HAW libraries , regardless of where you borrowed the book. Reserving and ordering library items How do I reserve a book from the HAW catalogue? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Do you want to borrow a book that another user has taken out of the library? You can reserve it using the HAW catalogue . The link for reserving a book is visible under 'loan status' for the item in question. As soon as the book is returned, we will reserve it for you. We will also inform you via email that it has been returned. You can then pick it up from the library, where it will be held for up to seven days. This service is free of charge. You may also cancel a reservation if you no longer need a particular item. How do I order a book from another HAW library? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Do you want to borrow a book from another HAW library but don't have time to pick it up yourself? It is possible to order it via the HAW catalogue . You can then pick it up at the HAW library of your choice roughly three days later. This service is free of charge. Please note that you can only order a maximum of five books at one time. Reference items and books that are currently on loan are excluded from this service. University Information and Library Service (HIBS) / last change 24.04.2026 x

title: Library Card
text: Library Card: HAW-Hamburg: Library card Libraries Using the libraries Library card (current) Your user account Fees Fill out the online registration form and submit it. If you do not have Internet access you can fill out the form at one of the HAW libraries. Come to one of the libraries and show us your ID card OR your passport and your residence certificate. If you are a member of HAW Hamburg (student, instructor, staff), please bring your chip card with you, if you have been issued a chip card. If you do not receive a chip card - From April 1, 2024, chip cards will no longer be issued to new students. Further information about Student ID card /chip card - please provide proof that you are enrolled or employed at HAW Hamburg. HAW Hamburg alumni need to have their alumni card. If you are entitled to a fee discount, please bring the necessary supporting documentation. If you are between 16 and 18 years old , you can obtain a library card if you bring a completed and signed declaration of consent from your parents. You can download the form here: declaration of parental consent (in German only) The following cards are used as a library card after the initial registration for library use: Chip card ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Members of HAW Hamburg who have received a chip card use it as a library card. Prior to this, you only have to register once for library use. You can find more information about this further up this page. Update from 21 March 2024: From April 1, 2024, chip cards will no longer be issued to new students. Further information about Student ID card /chip card The existing chip cards remain valid and should continue to be used as library cards . This applies both to the chip cards that have already been activated as library cards and to those that you will activate for library use. HAW Hamburg members without a chip card who would like to use the HAW libraries will receive a HIBS library card from us . Prior to this, a one-time initial registration is required for library use. You can find more information about this further up this page. AlumniCard ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Since 2009, all HAW Hamburg alumni can use their AlumniCard as a library card. To be activated for library use, please come to one of the HAW libraries with your AlumniCard and your ID card or passport (with your residence certificate). If you already have/had a HIBS library card or if your chip card was activated for library use, please bring it with you as well. Further information about AlumniCard (in German) The AlumniCard, which has been activated for the use of the HAW libraries, does not entitle the holder to use the other academic libraries in Hamburg. HIBS library card ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If you don't have a HAW chip card or an AlumniCard, we will provide you with a HIBS library card. Prior to this, a one-time initial registration

title: Library Card
text: Library Card: is required for library use. You can find more information about this further up this page. The annual or monthly fees you pay to the University Information and Library Service entitle you to borrow at no additional cost from the following academic libraries in Hamburg during the same period (you may need to also obtain a library card from the indvidual library): Hamburg State and University Library (SUB) Hamburg University of Technology Library (TUB) HafenCity University Library Library cards with reduced fee Students of public universities in the European Higher Education Area; students, staff and guests of public Hamburg universities, the Helmut Schmidt University - University of the Federal Armed Forces Hamburg or the Protestant University for Social Work & Diakonie in HamburgHAW Hamburg alumni No charge Secondary school students, teachers from Hamburg's public schools, apprentices No charge Individuals currently completing an apprenticeship, military service or volunteer civil service; recipients of Arbeitslosengeld I or II unemployment benefits (annual library card) €5.55 To receive a reduced fee, you must present one of the following: HAW Hamburg chip card or student card Student card from a public university in Germany HAW Hamburg employee ID HAW Hamburg alumni card Proof of another justification for a fee reduction, such as the receipt of state benefits pursuant to SGB II (ALG II) and SGB XII or employment in an apprenticeship position, military service or volunteer civil service Library cards without reduced fee Annual library card €44.50 Monthly library card €16.65 Annual library card for law firms and legal organisations €177.60 Please pay the fees using your EC (debit) card. Library cards without reduced fees are valid for one year, six months, or one month, depending on the price. Library cards with reduced fees are generally issued for one year. Newly issued library cards or chip cards for HAW Hamburg students are valid for four years. They can then be extended for one year upon presentation of a valid chip card. Alumni cards are activated for library use for one year at a time. To activate your card for an additional year, please present your alumni card and your personal ID or passport (together with your residence certificate) in one of the libraries. We will send you an automatic reminder that your library card is about to expire by email so that you can renew it on time. To do this, we must have your current email address. To renew your card, please bring the necessary documents to one of our libraries. These documents, and the renewal fee, will generally be the same as what was required for the initial registration. University Information and Library Service (HIBS) / last change 07.05.2026 x

title: Computers and Printing
text: Computers and Printing: Campus Library Finkenau at Finkenau 35. To give a presentation, simply connect your laptop to the large monitor using ClickShare. Detailed instructions are available on site at each CleverTouch monitor. Alternatively, you can connect via HDMI cable. title: Research Resources
text: Research Resources: HAW-Hamburg: Research resources Libraries Research resources Catalogues e-Journals Databases Standards Theses HAW Catalogue The HAW Catalogue is the main search tool for our books, journals, e-books and many other items. Can't find what you're looking for? Suggesting a book or journal purchase The HAW Hamburg's University Information and Library Service “HIBS” offers all HAW members free access to a wide range of electronic resources, both on campus and off-campus. However, due to a technical transition currently underway, external users are unfortunately unable to access our e-resources at present. The DIN standards are an exception to this. The extended access rights, such as access via VPN or Shibboleth, apply exclusively to members of the HAW Hamburg community (students, teaching staff and staff). Please also refer to the descriptions of the individual services in this regard! Accessing electronic materials while on campus ................................................................................................................................................................................................ All of the resources licensed for use as part of the University Information and Library Service are available at no cost in the HAW libraries and usually also via the HAW Hamburg academic network. This means that you can also access most electronic items via eduroam and in many HAW Hamburg laboratories. The most direct way to access our electronic materials is via the HAW Catalogue . For technical reasons, it is unfortunately not possible to access these resources via the HAW Hamburg administrative network, either directly or via a VPN connection. Accessing electronic materials remotely ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Most of the electronic materials licensed for use as part of the University Information and Library Service can also be accessed remotely if you are a HAW Hamburg member and log in to the HAW Hamburg academic network using a VPN connection . More information about VPN access Another possibility is to access materials using Shibboleth access . To do this you log in using your HAW account and password , either to the Shibboleth portal at https://login.haw-hamburg.de or directly on the publisher website from your search result in the HAW Catalogue. It is not necessary to install a VPN client in this case. You can find more information about Shibboleth below at “Shibboleth FAQs”. Some materials are also freely available on the Internet. You can access these items from anywhere at any time without restrictions. PDF files ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Many full-text documents in the Digital Library are available as PDF files. You will need Acrobat Reader to view PDF documents. If you do not already have this programme installed, you can download it for free here. Shibboleth FAQs What can I use Shibboleth for? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ When you work or study at home, you can access the HAW Hamburg website and some general university services via the Internet. But because some special HAW Hamburg services (e.g. databanks or e-journals) are only accessible via the internal HAW Hamburg network due to licensing issues, you can't access them directly. Until now, the only possible solution was to connect to the HAW Hamburg academic network via a VPN connection. Another possibility is to access materials using

title: Research Resources
text: Research Resources: Shibboleth access . To do this you log in using your HAW account and password , either to the Shibboleth portal at https://login.haw-hamburg.de or directly on the publisher website. It is not necessary to install a VPN client in this case. Shibboleth can be used to access those publishers listed below under 'Which publishers and resources are currently available via Shibboleth?' Who can use Shibboleth? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ All members of HAW Hamburg (students, instructors, employees) can generally use Shibboleth to access the licensed services. How do I log in via Shibboleth? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The simplest way is to log in via the Shibboleth portal at https://login.haw-hamburg.de using your HAW account and password . You can then go directly to the website of the publisher you want to use. You can also access the full-text items from the publisher directly via the links listed in your HAW Catalogue search results. Alternatively, you can go directly to the electronic resource you wish to use (databank, e-journal, e-book, etc.) without signing in beforehand. Some providers will then immediately direct you to a login window. In other cases you first need to select your country (or for Germany 'DFN-AAI') and the Hamburg University of Applied Sciences from a drop-down menu. You can then log in with your HAW account and password . In some cases you will also need to enter your HAW Hamburg email address. Which publishers and resources are already available via Shibboleth? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Databases and publishers that can be accessed via the institutional login (Shibboleth): ACM digital library Beck eLibrary Bloomsbury Collections Brockhaus Enzyklopädie Cambridge University Press Content-Select (Preselect) De Gruyter online Duncker & Humblot Ebook Central ProQuest (EBC) Elsevier Sciencedirect Emerald Insight Hogrefe eLibrary IEEE Xplore digital library Kohlhammer eLibrary Nomos eLibrary Römpp Online (Georg Thieme Verlag KG) Sage Publishing Springer Nature Statista Taylor & Francis eLibrary UTB elibrary Wiley online WISO-net (Genios) Alternatively, you can continue to access these publishers’ resources via VPN. Please note that access to e-resources via VPN or Shibboleth is restricted to members of the HAW Hamburg community (students, teaching staff and staff). Do I also need to log in when I am on campus? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Once you have logged in to a PC that is part of the campus network or used your mobile device to log in to HAW Hamburg's Wi-Fi network you no longer need to log in to Shibboleth. Who do I contact if I am unable to log in? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If you have difficulty logging in, please send an email to HIBS_Service (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de University Information and Library Service (HIBS) / last change 09.04.2026 x

title: Student Counselling Office
text: Student Counselling Office: HAW-Hamburg: Student Counselling Office The HAW Hamburg Student Counselling Office is an information and advising service at HAW Hamburg. As the university's student counsellors, we are your first point of contact for questions about our degree courses and in the event of personal problems or crises that negatively impact your studies. Our central responsibility is to provide you with information, support and advice before, at the beginning of, during and upon completion of your studies. We support you in your decision-making processes and encourage you to act independently. We also help you to develop strategies for dealing more effectively with issues or problems and to identify potential alternatives or other perspectives. Your individual abilities, skills, values, interests and needs are always at the centre of our actions. Our core values involve treating you with empathy, respect and appreciation and without prejudice. Our counsellors have university degrees, extensive training as counsellors and several years of professional experience, which enables us to address your concerns with a high level of professionalism. We ensure the quality of our counselling and advising services through regular professional development activities. Our discussions with you are provided free of charge and are strictly confidential. title: Psychological Support
text: Psychological Support: HAW-Hamburg: Psychological support Advising Student counselling and coaching Psychological support (current) Studying with a disability or chronic illness Conflicts and discrimination Studying towards a degree can present you with lots of challenges, and sometimes problems as well, which you might prefer to discuss with an external person who isn't involved. These issues may be study related – such as problems with motivation, learning disorders or (exam) anxiety – or they might have to do with personal conflicts in relationships, your family or your interactions with other students. That's what we're here for. You can come to us with any personal problem. Thanks to our years of experience counselling students, we are especially familiar with the problems that can arise. Our goal is to help you overcome and resolve the issues and conflicts that are troubling you. In an initial discussion we clarify what is presenting you with problems and what support you need to resolve the issue in question. There could be up to five follow-up appointments, or we might support you in finding a therapist or a suitable counselling centre to provide longer-term treatment. Counselling appointments are free of charge for all HAW Hamburg students. All employees are bound by confidentiality agreements, which means that all discussions are absolutely confidential. Student Counselling Office Student Center Stiftstraße 69, 'Haus 1', 1st floor 20099 Hamburg T +49 151 7281 8022 studienberatung (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de Telephone office hours: Mon and Tues: 9:00 am–10:00 am Mon and Thurs: 4:00 pm–5:00 pm In-person office hours: Mon and Tues: 11:00 am–1:00 pm Appointments ................................................................................................................................................................................................ We also offer psychological counselling sessions by appointment. Please send a brief mail with your telephone number to studienberatung(@)haw-hamburg.de . We will contact you as quickly as possible. Crisis support outside our office hours ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Here are some of the organisations you can contact in a crisis situation: Student counselling helpline operated by the Hamburg Protestant Students Association daily from 8:00 pm to 12:00 am : +49 40 411 70 411 Counselling helpline (day and night): 0800 1110111 Psychiatric outpatient departments at local hospitals, e.g. Acute Psychiatric Outpatient Department at the Schön Klinik, Dehnhaide 120, Tel: +49 40 2092-1410, nights: +49 40 2092-2911 Hamburg emergency medical service: +49 40 228022 Paths to psychotherapy ................................................................................................................................................................................................ We do not offer psychotherapy, but we do provide advice on all questions related to out-patient psychotherapy and support you in your search for a suitable therapy setting. You can find additional information about psychotherapy and looking for a psychotherapist on the websites of the Hamburg Chamber of Psychotherapists and the Information Service of the Professional Association of German Psychologists. title: Student Health – Programmes & Activities
text: Student Health – Programmes & Activities: HAW-Hamburg: Our programmes and activities Student health Our programmes and activities Mental Health First Aid Supporting healthy studies at HAW Hamburg - Take part! A wide variety of factors influence student well-being at the university. These can be both individual and structural. In addition to information on health-related questions that have to do, for example, with exercise or eating habits, CamPuls also aims to deliver information about social, sexual, pyschological and other issues. In consultation with students and with the help of surveys, the progammes are adjusted and further developed on an ongoing basis. Are specific topics missing? Feel free to send us your ideas anytime at campuls (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de Input and information for everyday student life is posted regularly on our Instagram account . Overview of services and events HEALTHYLAND Festival ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The 2023 HEALTHYLAND Festival is coming up soon. It will take place from 22 to 24 May. In the various workshops and presentations, you can get tips and tricks for making your everyday student life healthier. We will also be looking at the role the university plays in your health. Together with other HAW Hamburg students and employees, you'll discover how much fun the topic of health can be. And of course, there will also be delicious snacks, drinks, games and a live concert too. Take a look . The detailed programme of events for the 2023 HEALTHYLAND Festival is available (in German) here . Mental health first-aiders ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Is there actually such a thing as first-aid for mental health? Yes, there is! And as of 2022, it's available at HAW Hamburg too: our mental health first-aiders are students themselves and are trained to recognise mental health problems and respond appropriately until professional help is available. They are located in all of the faculties and are the contact people for students experiencing personal crises or problems. Scroll down to get to know the mental health first-aiders in the various faculties. Places to study, relax and meet up ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Hamburg is a big port city! This means that quiet and peaceful places to study, relax and meet up aren't always so easy to find. This why the CamPuls team set out to find them. Student assistants Sigrid, Kim and Henry have discovered lots of restorative parks and lakes, as well as good libraries for studying. Now it's time for you to visit them. If you think the best spots are still missing, we're happy to add to the places outlined below: campuls (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de . Please note that turning on the video will result in the transmission of data to the third-party provider. Turn on video. Getting exercise and consciously taking time

Talking with my fellow students and breaks for movement and relaxation Why did you become a first-aider? It's important to me to be able to support people who are in difficult situations. I also want to contribute to reducing the stigma around mental illnesses in our society. © Stella Kaltenbach Stella Kaltenbach Health Sciences stella.kaltenbach@haw-hamburg.de What helps you stay healthy? Since I'm originally from the Black Forest, I love to go hiking. Being in nature helps me relax and de-stress. Dance and yoga are also important parts of my everyday life. Why did you become a first-aider? From personal experience, I know that there are sometimes periods that are particularly tiring and challenging. Everyone should have the opportunity to talk to someone who listens to them and shows them understanding in crisis situations. As a first-aider I want to contribute to making it possible for people to speak openly about crisis situations without being afraid of being stigmatised. Faculty of Design, Media and Information © CamPuls Benjamin Possin Media and Communications benjamin.possin@haw-hamburg.de What helps you stay healthy during your studies? To keep myself healthy I look for regular activities that balance out the work and where I can get away from university-related topics and immerse myself in another world with friends, such as regular games nights or going to movies. © CamPuls_HAW Erika Rausch Communication Design erika.rausch@haw-hamburg.de What helps you stay healthy during your studies? Dancing destresses me and helps me relax physically and mentally. Hanna Steinhoff Costume Design hanna.steinhoff@haw-hamburg.de What helps you stay healthy during your studies? 'Mindfulness' and 'balancing' are important keywords for me! I've now developed a strong awareness of my body and spirit and quickly notice and take seriously any signs of stress or being overwhelmed. To get through stressful phases, I try to always maintain balance between obligations and free time or relaxation. Why did you become a first-aider? I've always found psychology and educational theory exciting and fascinating, which is why I am getting training in this area. Being able to understand, and even support, myself and other people better gives me a

Why did you become a first-aider? I took the course because I had already volunteered at a telephone service that involved students listening to students and I noticed that more knowledge about mental health issues, support offers and resources is very important, and that many people impacted can't or don't want to speak with those in their direct environment. This is why I think it's super that this course is being offered at HAW Hamburg and that there's an easy-to-access service for students who are affected! Time with good friends for balance, lots of music and good food help me stay healthy as I study. :) Why did you become a first-aider? I became a mental health first-aider to give others a safe

title: Student Health – Programmes & Activities
text: Student Health – Programmes & Activities: space for mental health. © CamPuls_HAW Immo Kutz Social Work immooliver.kutz@haw-hamburg.de What helps you stay healthy during your studies? Having my family and hobbies as a safe place to return to! Why did you become a first-aider? I became a mental health first-aider to do my best to provide all my fellow humans with first-aid for the diverse mental health stressors that exist in an increasingly complex world. My credo is based on Ghandi's words: Be the change you want to see in the world = to live each day with compassion for my fellow humans. © CamPuls_Haw Katharina Baum Social Work katharina.baum@haw-hamburg.de What helps you stay healthy during your studies? After stress comes relaxation; after we breathe out, we breathe in. © CamPuls_HAW Yannik Thieß Social Work yannik.thiess@haw-hamburg.de What helps you stay healthy during your studies? Just leaving things unfinished at times and simply enjoying the evening with friends. If I need more time, I can ask for it. © CamPuls_haw Max Holz Education and Learning in Childhood max.holz@haw-hamburg.de What helps you stay healthy during your studies? Exercise and activities with friends Why did you become a first-aider? To support friends with depression © CamPuls Astrid Rösch Social Work astrid.roesch@haw-hamburg.de What helps you stay healthy? Paying attention to what I need, balancing work with socialising, hobbies, etc., and lots of self-care. And for me, tea is always a symbol of looking out for myself and taking a break. Why did you become a first-aider? Mental health problems are much more common than we might think, and lots of people are impacted at some point in their life. By taking the course, I wanted to contribute to reducing the stigma around mental health problems and get training to support those impacted in the best way possible, to be there for them, and to research suitable next steps together with them. Hamburg is a big port city! This means that quiet and peaceful places to study, relax and meet up aren't always so easy to find. This why the CamPuls team set out to find them. Student assistants Sigrid, Kim and Henry have discovered lots of restorative parks and lakes, as well as good libraries for studying. Now it's time for you to visit them. If you think the best spots are still missing, we're happy to add to the places outlined below: campuls (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de . title: Student Health – Programmes & Activities
text: Student Health – Programmes & Activities: ice arena With a surface of 4,300 square metres, the Hamburg ice arena is one of the largest outdoor skating rinks in the world. It is located in the Planten un Blomen park between Hamburg Neustadt and St. Pauli, and is definitely worth visiting with friends! See map © HENRY JANOWITZ Elbstrand A beach right outside your door! The beach along the Elbe stretches for roughly 13 kilometres downriver towards the river mouth and offers a good view of the port. There are lots of places where you can sit comfortably in the sand and watch the big container ships go by. See map Entenwerder The Elbpark Entenwerder is a public park on the Entenwerder peninsula in the northern arm of the Elbe, in the Hamburg neighbourhood of Rothenburgsort. The cafe right on the water makes it an attractive destination! See map The Boberger dunes In the Boberger lowlands, a nature reserve located between the Bille River and Bergedorfer Straße, you can take a walk through Hamburg's last remaining dunes. It's a super place to take a break and get away from it all! See map Hanging Out Cafe Here you can find a large selection of vegetarian and vegan food. The tropical atmosphere makes it a perfect place to forget the stresses of everyday life. See map Hayns Park This pleasant and forested park is located in Eppendorf. It is perfect for picnics, barbecues, games and boat trips. You'll also be able to find cake! See map Henneberg Park Another park in Hamburg worth visiting, in the neighbourhood Poppenbüttel. See map © HENRY JANOWITZ Jenisch Park There's almost no other place in Hamburg that's so natural, so expansive and so relaxing. When you're there in its fields and forests, you might forget that you're in a city of almost two million people. See map Ohlsdorf Cemetery An unusual place to relax: the huge Ohlsdorf Cemetery. Things that are strictly forbidden at other cemeteries are possible here. Cars, bikes and joggers are a completely normal sight. See map © HENRY JANOWITZ Park Fiction Park Fiction, officially called Antonipark, is a sociopolitical art project. Anyone looking for a small oasis of green alongside the Fish Market on the Elbe will find it here. See map Planten un Blomen This park's name, which is from the Plattdeutsch (Low German) dialect, means plants and flowers. It is home to a botanical garden with a tropical greenhouse and the largest Japanese garden in Europe. See map The Pony Bar Here you'll find a relaxed atmosphere with yummy drinks, live music and a foosball table. It's a place tailored to young people. See map The Social Sciences Library at the Universität Hamburg You'll find this perfect place to study near the Zoological Museum. See map Stadtpark With its roughly 150 hectares, the Stadtpark (City Park) in Winterhude is the third-largest greenspace in the Hanseatic city. Not only is it a great place to meet up and relax, but music lovers can also listen in on

title: Food and Drink on Campus
text: Food and Drink on Campus: HAW-Hamburg: Food and drink Campus life Food and drink (current) Culture and sports Student health Accommodation Student projects Students' Union and Student Parliament Choir Campus locations Every HAW Hamburg campus has a cafeteria, and also often student cafes. The cafeterias are run by Studierendenwerk Hamburg . They offer good and inexpensive meals for students: €1.20 for soup, and main dishes from €1.85 to €4.95. Studierendenwerk Hamburg places great value on providing healthy food in the cafeterias and offers vegetarian meals on a regular basis and salads on a daily basis. They also sell delicious desserts, muffins and coffee. title: Campus Bergedorf
text: Campus Bergedorf: HAW-Hamburg: Campus Bergedorf Am Campus Bergedorf im Stadtteil Lohbrügge seid ihr gut angebunden an die Innenstadt und findet alles direkt vor Ort. Hier erwarten dich acht Bachelor und neun Master-Studiengänge aus drei Fakultäten, moderne Hörsäle, Labore, eine Bibliothek, Mensa, Café sowie vielfältige Lern- und Freizeitmöglichkeiten. Nur zwei Stationen entfernt ist das Wohnheim Allermöhe vom Studierendenwerks Hamburg . Seit 2015 steht das Technologiezentrum Energie-Campus des Competence Center für Erneuerbare Energien und EnergieEffizienz ( CC4E ) in Bergedorf. Hier findest du modernste Forschungseinrichtungen wie das Windlabor, das Smart-Grid-Labor sowie den 2017 errichteten Windpark Curslack – einen einzigartigen Lern- und Forschungsort für die Energiewende. Fakultäten am Campus Bergedorf Fakultät Life Sciences Fakultät Gesundheit Fakultät Nachhaltige Ingenieurwissenschaften Campusleben Draußen : Auf der großen Mensaterrasse kannst du essen, lernen oder einfach entspannen. Dazu gibt’s Tische, Bänke, Liegen – und ein Beachvolleyballfeld für die aktive Pause. Drinnen : Ob Schach, Kicker, Billard oder Tischtennis – hier kannst du gemeinsam mit anderen eine Auszeit vom Lernen nehmen. Bälle, Schläger und Queues bekommst du ganz unkompliziert beim Fachschaftsrat.
