title: International Students – Finances
text: International Students – Finances: HAW-Hamburg: Finances International students Funding and scholarships Finances (current) Scholarships We have answered some of the most common questions our international students ask about the cost of living and studying in Hamburg and how they can fund their studies. How much money do I need as a student in Hamburg? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Most HAW Hamburg degree courses (with a few exceptions, such as the Master of Public Health*) do not charge tuition fees . You will only have to pay a ‘semester contribution’ ( Semesterbeitrag ) of €397 per semester. About half of this sum is for your semester ticket , which allows you to use public transport services throughout the HVV area. The remainder is shared among the AStA (the student representative body), the Studierendenwerk and HAW Hamburg itself, and is used to cover various administrative expenses. Studying itself isn’t expensive, but living in Hamburg can be. Hamburg is Germany’s second-largest city and among those with the highest average cost of living. Here’s an overview of the typical average expenses you can expect to have to cover as a student (they will, of course, vary according to the lifestyle you choose): Item/expense Cost Rent in a student dormitory or shared apartment (including heating, utilities, internet) €350–€750 / month Food €200–€350 / month (Mobile) phone €10–€40 / month Statutory health insurance (student rate) €145 / month Personal liability insurance ( Haftpflichtversicherung ) €7 / month Books, stationery and study supplies €0–€50 / month Transport covered by your semester contribution Exercise and sports €10–€50 / month Other expenses (e.g. clothing, going out, hobbies) €100-200/ month Lunch in the canteen (Mensa) €3–€7 A Milchkaffee (coffee with frothed milk) €1.50–€5 1 packet of rice €0.70–€4 1 carton of milk €0.90–€3 As a student in Hamburg, you will need about €992 per month to have a reasonable lifestyle (not extravagant, but not extremely frugal). How can I finance my studies? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Ideally, you will have saved up some money before coming to Hamburg, or secured a scholarship, or your family will provide you with financial support. However if you are funding your studies, we recommend you come to Hamburg with money to last you at least a year (about €10,000). Most students take on paid work alongside their studies. The jobs most frequently done by students are part-time casual roles (for example, in shops, as waiting staff or bartenders, or as delivery staff), or work as student assistants in their institute or faculty. None of these jobs pay a great deal of money, and it can be hard to find a job when you’re in your first two semesters, especially if your German isn’t very advanced yet. Don’t forget that you will need a lot of time for your studies – usually around 40 hours per week. This means that the more paid work you do alongside, the less time you will have for your academic work, and your grades may even suffer. In later semesters, when you’ve gained some knowledge and experience, you may

title: International Students – Finances
text: International Students – Finances: be able to find a job in a company connected to your field – as what’s called a Werkstudent*in – or apply for a scholarship for high-achieving students. However you meet these challenges, it’s always the case that studying gets harder if you find yourself having to worry about money on top of everything else. There are also important rules that international students need to be aware of. Students from non-EU countries are allowed to take on paid work of up to 20 hours a week, to a maximum total of 140 full days or 280 half-days in each calendar year. In semester breaks (non-teaching periods), you are permitted to exceed the 20-hour-a-week limit, but the 140-day limit still applies. Make sure you document the hours and days you work and stick to the limits – otherwise you and/or your employer could end up in trouble with the authorities. Are there any scholarships for international students? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The International Office awards scholarships to high-achieving international students, and/or those who spend time volunteering to help others. You have to be at least in your third semester to be eligible as a Bachelor’s degree student and at least in your second semester if you are doing a Master’s course. We also award grants to students in the final phase of their degree courses. Visit our scholarships page to find out more. The HAW Hamburg is also a participant in the Deutschlandstipendium programme. There are numerous other foundations and organisations which award financial support to international students. You can search for opportunities in scholarship databases, like this one: https://www.stipendienlotse.de/ What if I find myself in financial difficulties? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Sometimes things don’t go to plan and you end up in unexpected situations. You might lose your job suddenly, or become ill and take some time to recover. These problems may cause you financial difficulties through no fault of your own. An association affiliated to HAW Hamburg, the Verein der Förderer Ausländische Studierende (VDFAS), can grant emergency financial help to international students in these situations, so you won’t have to starve, lose your home or be forced to give up your degree. You can get help here . €200 and €500 notes: a word of warning ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Unfortunately, €200 and €500 notes are often forged (faked). Even when they are real, you’ll find it very difficult to use them, because most staff in shops won’t accept them. We urgently recommend that you avoid these notes. If you use money transfer services like MoneyGram and Western Union, make sure you insist on being given smaller-value notes (up to €100). Team International / last change 22.05.2026 x

title: Excellence Scholarships (International)
text: Excellence Scholarships (International): HAW-Hamburg: Excellence scholarships Funding and scholarships Scholarships Excellence scholarships (current) Scholarships for exceptional social commitment Final Year Scholarships DAAD Prize Scholarships from external funders Hamburg University of Applied Sciences (HAW Hamburg) awards scholarships to high-achieving international Bachelor’s students from the third semester onwards and to international Master’s students from the second semester onwards. The scholarships are funded by the Free and Hanseatic City of Hamburg. Attention: The application option for this round will be activated starting March 1, 2026. Applications created before this date cannot be considered and may need to be resubmitted. Thank you for your understanding. If you have any questions regarding the merit-based scholarships, please contact: io_scholarships@haw-hamburg.de. FAQs Application criteria ................................................................................................................................................................................................ You may apply for a merit-based scholarship if you meet the following criteria: You are enrolled as an international student at HAW Hamburg (full-time and/or part-time). You obtained your higher education entrance qualification (HZB) abroad or have successfully completed a German Studienkolleg. You have successfully studied at HAW Hamburg for at least two semesters (Bachelor’s) or one semester (Master’s) and are within the standard period of study for your degree programme. You do not receive BAföG or any other state benefits. Your average grade is at least 2.2. (Not eligible for funding are students enrolled in fee-based or continuing education programmes; students enrolled in a joint and/or double degree programme who are entitled to funding from other sponsors; students who are entitled to funding from other sponsors, receive funding from other sponsors, and/or already receive other funding from HAW Hamburg; students who are eligible for benefits under the German Federal Training Assistance Act (BAföG); and international students with dual citizenship, one of which is German.) Duration and amount of funding ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Merit-based scholarships are awarded in the amount of at least €1,800 for one semester . In this application round, funding will be granted for the Summer Semester 2026 . The scholarship will be paid out in instalments. Application deadlines ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Summer semester: 30 March of each year Winter semester: 30 September of each year Attention: The application option for this round will be activated starting March 1, 2026. Applications created before this date cannot be considered and may need to be resubmitted. Thank you for your understanding. Letter of recommendation and other application documents ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If you are applying for a merit-based scholarship at HAW Hamburg for the first time, you will need recommendation letters of two (2!) lecturers from your degree programme. Please use the current reference form for the Summer Semester 2026: Gutachtenformular Recommendation letter . Master’s students who have already received a merit-based scholarship during their Bachelor’s studies must also submit two current references. Other students for whom two current references are already on file (e.g. from a previous application) do not need to submit new ones. However, when applying via Mobility Online, you must provide detailed information (names of referees, relevant semester, etc.). The references should not be older than 12 months. Important: A recommendation letter must be requested from a

title: Excellence Scholarships (International)
text: Excellence Scholarships (International): lecturer who is very familiar with your academic performance. You may already approach a lecturer now to ask whether they would be willing to provide a reference and inform them in advance. Once the application period begins, please download the reference form and fill in your personal details beforehand. You can then forward the current, pre-filled form to your referees. Please ask the lecturers to send their references with their assessment directly to: io_scholarship@haw-hamburg.de . Please do not submit more than two references. In addition, the following documents must be submitted via Mobility Online : Current CV (max. 2 pages) (obligatory) Current Certificate of enrolment from myHAW (obligatory) Current transcript of records from myHAW with information about average grade and completed credit points (obligatory) and, if applicable, an overview of exam registrations For students whose average grade and credit points are not listed in their transcript (this currently applies to all degree courses in the Faculty of Engineering and Computer Science, with the exception of Automotive Engineering, Aeronautical Engineering and Mechanical Engineering), the International Office will request the average grade and credit points from the FSB after the application deadline. Copy of your residency permit (non-EU citizens) or a copy of your passport or personal ID card (EU citizens) (obligatory) Bank account information form (obligatory) Financial circumstances form (obligatory) Motivation letter, other documents or explanations (optional, this is not assessed!). Submitting your application ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Please allow sufficient time for your application and make sure to read all information carefully. Collect all required documents and upload them to our application portal ( Mobility Online ) before the application deadline. The link will be provided here shortly. Please remember that the letters of recommendation must be sent separately from your application by the referees to io_scholarships (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de . As a rule, you will be informed about the outcome of your application within four to six weeks after the application deadline. The International Office of HAW Hamburg wishes you every success with your application! Additional FAQs Residency permits ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Which residency permit do I need to have in order to apply for an excellence scholarship? Students with residency status pursuant to section 16 of the federal residency act (residency for the purpose of study) and EU citizens are eligible to apply for the scholarships issued by the International Office, if they are not eligible for BAföG or other state funding. International students who are refugees cannot apply for these scholarships. International students with a different residency status can only apply in exceptional cases and following clarification of their individual situation. Foreign students with dual citizenship, one of which is German, are not eligible to apply. I currently have only a temporary residence permit ( Fiktionsbescheinigung ). Can I still apply? Yes. Selection process ................................................................................................................................................................................................ How are the scholarship recipients selected? Once all of the applications have been reviewed, applicants will be allocated points on the basis of their average grades, the average number of credit points per semester, and the

title: Excellence Scholarships (International)
text: Excellence Scholarships (International): letters of reference. This ranking will serve as a guide for the selection committee. Other criteria such as students' financial situation or whether they have already received the scholarship will also be considered. In the case of students who have already received a scholarship, the committee will assess whether their performance has improved relative to the previous application. When can I expect a decision? You can generally expect to hear about the outcome of your application within four weeks of the deadline. BAföG (student financial aid) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Am I eligible for an excellence scholarship if I am receiving BAföG (student financial aid)? No. When are international students eligible to receive BAföG? You are eligible to receive BAföG if you fulfil the criteria listed in section 8 of the federal law on student financial assistance (BAföG) . If you are uncertain whether you meet these criteria, please contact the Student Financial Aid Advising Centre before submitting your application. You should be sure to do this in the following cases: You do not have a residency permit under section 16 of the Aufenthaltsgesetz (the federal residency act). You are an EU citizen and you have a student job or worked in Germany before your studies. You are married to a German citizen who is working in Germany. Application criteria ................................................................................................................................................................................................ I was placed in the third semester but have only studied at HAW Hamburg for one semester so far. Can I still apply for a scholarship? No, unfortunately not. You must have studied at HAW Hamburg for at least two semesters and be able to demonstrate your performance. I am currently completing an interim semester (I am enrolled for a Master's degree course but am still writing my Bachelor's thesis). Can I apply for a scholarship? No. In this case you can apply for a degree completion grant . I am studying for a Master of Public Health or the European Master of Medical Technology and Healthcare Business. Can I apply for a scholarship? Unfortunately not. Students in continuing professional education or fee-based degree courses as well as students in joint or double degree courses are excluded from receiving support. My average grade is 2.29. Do I fulfil the application criteria? Yes. Only the first number following the decimal point is taken into account. Multiple scholarships ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Am I permitted to receive other scholarships in addition to an excellence scholarship? Receiving other scholarships in addition to an excellence scholarship is not permitted. However, you can apply for multiple scholarships in order to increase your chances of receiving funding. Motivation or voluntary commitment ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Does my motivation or voluntary commitment play a role in applying for a merit-based scholarship? You may submit an optional letter of motivation or other explanations. However, it is NOT an assessment criterion (!). Voluntary commitment is also NOT a criterion that is evaluated for a merit-based scholarship. However, academic staff may take subject-related engagement — for example, in projects or at academic events — into account in their

title: Excellence Scholarships (International)
text: Excellence Scholarships (International): academic letters of recommendation. If you have undertaken international activities on a volunteer basis at HAW Hamburg, you can apply for a scholarship for exceptionally socially committed students . Financial situation ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Why do I have to indicate my financial situation in my application? If you are supporting yourself financially (not receiving support from your parents, spouse or friends), you can receive extra points for your application depending on your income. Maximum funding duration ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Is there a limit to how often I can receive the scholarship? You can receive an excellence scholarship for maximum 2 years (4 semesters). However, other criteria such as the total number of applications are considered in the respective selection process. Additionally, you can apply for a scholarship for highly committed students and for a Degree Completion Grant at Studierendenwerk Hamburg. Recommendation letter ................................................................................................................................................................................................ How many letters of recommendation do I need? For your first application, you need letters of recommendation from two lecturers in your degree program. If you are reapplying within the same degree program, you do not need new letters as long as the existing ones are not older than 12 months. If you received funding during your Bachelor’s studies and are applying again for a scholarship during your Master’s studies, you will need two new and current letters of recommendation. When should I request the letters of recommendation? Request the letters as early as possible, ideally after the last examination period and at least two weeks before the respective application deadline. Lecturers may decline to issue a letter of recommendation if you request it too late. Whom should I ask? It is best to approach professors or lecturers who are able to assess your academic performance well — for example, because you actively participated in their courses or projects. How are the letters of recommendation submitted? Once the application period begins, please download the recommendation form ( Gutachtenformular Recommendation letter ) and fill in your personal details. You can then send your referees the relevant information along with the current form. Please ask the lecturers to send their completed letters directly to io_scholarship (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de . Can I submit more than two letters of recommendation? Please only request the required two letters, as asking for more would create unnecessary additional work for the lecturers. Transcript of Records (Grades) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ What should I do if some of my exam results have not been recorded in my online transcript? Please contact the instructor or professor in question and ask them to publish their exam results. If all of the exam results have still not been published by the application deadline, please submit an overview of the exams you were registered for. If you completed an internship in the previous semester that has not yet been entered, you can also submit a letter of confirmation from the company where you completed the internship. I did not receive any grades last semester, or in my degree course the grades won't be assessed/entered until

title: Excellence Scholarships (International)
text: Excellence Scholarships (International): a subsequent semester. Can I still apply? Because your study achievements are an important assessment criteria for your application, you cannot apply if you don't have any grades for the previous semester in your transcript. In this case you should contact your professors and ask if it is possible for your work to be assessed earlier and your grades entered. Mobility Online ................................................................................................................................................................................................ What is Mobility Online? Mobility Online is the application portal where you must log in using your HAW ID (HAW-W-Kennung) in order to apply for a scholarship. After registering there, you can upload your application documents to the portal. When will Mobility Online be activated? As a rule, the portal opens four weeks before the respective application deadline — in the upcoming round, likely on March 1, 2026. How much time should I plan for the application? Please register in Mobility Online as early as possible, at least one week before the application deadline. This ensures that, in case of technical issues or questions of understanding, you still have enough time to contact the International Office before the deadline. Standard period of study (Regelstudienzeit) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ What is the 'standard period of study' ( Regelstudienzeit )? The standard period of study is the amount of time allocated for the completion of your degree course (for Bachelor's degree courses at HAW Hamburg it is six or seven semesters; for Master's degree courses it is three or four semesters). As long as you haven't surpassed this number (of subject semesters), you are still within the standard period of study, regardless of how many credit points you have completed. What counts is the standard period of study (generelle Regelstudienzeit) as indicated on your enrolment certificate. I have exceeded the standard period of study. Can I still apply for a scholarship? If you fulfil the criteria, you can apply for a scholarship for exceptionally socially committed international students or a degree completion grant . Legal Notice ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Funding is granted only within the limits of available budgetary resources; there is no legal entitlement to funding. Loans are not awarded. With a merit-based scholarship, international students may be funded if they are enrolled in a Bachelor’s or Master’s degree program at HAW Hamburg and have obtained their higher education entrance qualification (HZB) abroad or at a German Studienkolleg (so-called Bildungsausländer innen*). The decisions of the selection committee will be communicated to applicants in writing (by email). Students must be informed of their obligation to notify the responsible office immediately of any changes to the information provided in their application and, if necessary, provide appropriate documentation. Hamburg University of Applied Sciences reserves the right to revoke funding approval if there is an important reason to do so. An important reason exists if the scholarship recipient: a) is no longer eligible for funding due to discontinuation of their studies, and/or b) has intentionally or negligently provided false or incomplete information about significant facts or has concealed important facts (e.g., simultaneous public funding from other sources).

title: Deutschlandstipendium – Germany Scholarship
text: Deutschlandstipendium – Germany Scholarship: HAW-Hamburg: Information for sponsors Scholarships Deutschlandstipendium (Germany Scholarship) Information for students Information for sponsors (current) Scholarship recipients Awards ceremonies The provision of financial support to particularly gifted and capable students at HAW Hamburg through the Deutschlandstipendium is only possible with the participation of corporate and individual sponsors. The idea behind the Deutschlandstipendium , which was established by the federal government in 2010, is simple: together with private sponsors, the federal government and the states support gifted students in order to provide them with the best possible conditions for their studies and thus their careers. Thank you to all of the sponsors who already support the Deutschlandstipendium at HAW Hamburg! How does the Deutschlandstipendium work? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Each scholarship is awarded for one year and is paid to the recipients in monthly installments of €300. Private sponsors fund half of each scholarship. The federal government and the states provide the other half. In concrete terms, this means that with a donation of only €150 per month (€1,800 per year) you are investing in a student's future. This can form the basis for other networking opportunities such as trainee programmes, practicum placements or final projects. HAW Hamburg acts an intermediary, organises the selection of the students, and manages the programme. The beginning of each funding period is marked with an awards ceremony at HAW Hamburg, which we invite all sponsors and scholarship recipients to attend. This official ceremony, which is usually held in March, provides the first opportunity to get to know one another in person. You can see photos of the last awards ceremony here . As part of an accompanying programme the university organises at least two main events, and all sponsors and scholarship recipients are invited to attend. How does the Deutschlandstipendium benefit you? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ By deciding to become a sponsor you can invest directly in a talented young person's academic education. Your participation in the Deutschlandstipendium programme also benefits you in various ways: You can establish contact with tomorrow's business leaders. We make it possible for you to meet the scholarship recipients in person in a festive setting. You can position yourself as an academic sponsor and use the Deutschlandstipendium logo. We honour your involvement on the HAW Hamburg website (if desired). You can claim the funding as a donation for tax purposes. Your sponsorship facilitates exchange with the university and can serve as a springboard for further cooperation. How can I become a sponsor? ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Sponsorship is based on your provision of financial support for the students. There are two ways of donating: Through an annual scholarship (min. €1,800) you take on the sponsorship of one or more students. In the funding agreement you can make specific funding requests – for example, that the funding be dedicated to a particular faculty or degree course. Through a one-time donation (min. €100) that flows directly into our scholarship fund, where we collect all donations for the Deutschlandstipendium . As soon as adequate funding for a scholarship is available, a student

title: Deutschlandstipendium – Germany Scholarship
text: Deutschlandstipendium – Germany Scholarship: will be selected. If you want to become a sponsor for the Deutschlandstipendium, then kindly contact us by using this e-mail: deutschlandstipendium@haw-hamburg.de We would be very pleased to welcome you as a Deutschlandstipendium sponsor at HAW Hamburg! Thank you to all of the companies, individuals, foundations, clubs and anonymous donors who support the Deutschlandstipendium at HAW Hamburg! At HAW Hamburg the Deutschlandstipendium is made possible by the following funders, amongst others: Frau Anne Gerhard und Herr Wolfgang Jacobsen-Gerhard Bonprix Handelsgesellschaft mbH Dr. Lothar Dittmer Karl Heinz Ditze Stiftung Esso Deutschland GmbH Dr. Heinrich und Erika Kreye E.W. title: Scholarships and Funding – Studying Abroad
text: Scholarships and Funding – Studying Abroad: HAW-Hamburg: Scholarships and funding Studying abroad Europe and Erasmus+ Studying overseas Double degrees Scholarships and funding (current) Application deadlines and applications Preparation and FAQs Summer schools and short-term programmes Student reports Recognition of study credits Here you can find the most important funding programmes that can help you to finance a semester abroad. Each programme has different application requirements and deadlines. The application deadlines can be up to one year before the start of your planned study period abroad, so please start your planning in good time. Erasmus+ (Europe) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Eramus+ is an educational programme run by the European Union which was initiated in 1987 to promote cross-border mobility of students within Europe. The programme enables you to study at a HAW Hamburg partner university in one of the EU states or one of the other European programme countries (Iceland, Liechtenstein, Macedonia, Norway, Serbia or Turkey). What is special about the Erasmus programme is that the awarding of a study place at a European partner university is accompanied by financial support through an Erasmus scholarship for at least two months per semester. This pairing of a study place and financial support makes the programme especially attractive for students. The amount of funding depends on the country where you spend your study semester and is based on the cost of living there. Funding amounts can vary from year to year. The minimum duration of funding per semester is two complete months. For stays in the 2022/23 academic year (WS2022/23 and SS2023), the following amounts apply: Country group 1 : Denmark, Finland, Ireland, Iceland, Liechtenstein, Luxembourg, Norway, Sweden: €600/month (€20/day) Country group 2 : Belgium, France, Greece, Italy, Malta, Netherlands, Austria, Portugal, Spain, Cyprus: €540/month (€18/day) Country group 3 : Bulgaria, North Macedonia, Estonia, Croatia, Latvia, Lithuania, Poland, Romania, Serbia, Slovakia, Slovenia, Czech Republic, Turkey, Hungary: €490/month (€16,33/day) You can find information about the Erasmus+ programme and applying for a study place/scholarship here . The programme also provides financial support for an internship stay of at least two months in another European country. PROMOS (USA) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Through the German Academic Exchange Service's (DAAD) PROMOS programme, the HAW Hamburg International Office awards scholarships for a study semester at a US partner university*. Students who are awarded a place as part of the HAW goes USA or Global E3 programme will automatically be considered for a PROMOS scholarship. An additional application is not necessary. If a selection process is necessary due to limited funding, this will take place on the basis of the rankings generated during the 'HAW goes USA'/Global E3 interviews. *Due to limited funding it is unfortunately not possible to offer scholarships to "freemovers". Scholarship requirements: You must successfully complete at least 12 US Bachelor credits (four "Fachkurse" of 3 US credits each) or 9 US Master credits (three "Fachkurse" of 3 US credits each) in the USA and submit a report of at least 10 pages at the end of the semester. Project year 2026: HAW Hamburg can award a maximum

title: Scholarships and Funding – Studying Abroad
text: Scholarships and Funding – Studying Abroad: of 25 travel grants - 1,350 euros (USA East) or 1,375 euros (USA West) for the programme year 2026. It may also be possible to award a partial scholarship of 2,200 euros. This depends on how many people in total are to be funded. A final decision will be made in mid-June 2026 when the decisions on the DAAD HAW.International scholarships have been awarded You will then receive further information from the International Office. PROMOS scholarships are transfered monthly from 1 September. Please note: If your application for a HAW.International scholarship from DAAD or an ISAP scholarship is successful, you cannot receive the PROMOS scholarship. Double funding is not possible. If your application for a Fulbright travel scholarship is successful, the PROMOS scholarship will be reduced by the amount of the travel subsidy. If you receive a DAAD or Fulbright scholarship, you are obligated to inform the International Office as soon as possible: Ingrid Weatherall ISAP-Scholarships (USA) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ HAW Hamburg receives ISAP funding from the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) for ISAP funding for scholarships for a semester abroad in the USA. HAW Hamburg awards the following scholarships: Virginia Tech (aerospace) – WS26/27-WS29/30 – four scholarships per year worth €8,525 each CSULB (business) - WS26/27-WS29/30 - three scholarships per year worth €8,550 each CSULB (social work) - SoSe27 & SoSe28 - three scholarships per year worth €8,550 each Scholarship conditions: You must successfully complete at least 12 US credits (four subject courses worth 3 US credits each) in the USA and submit a report of at least 10 pages at the end of the semester. ISAP-Scholarships (China) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Until winter semester 2027/28, outstanding HAW Hamburg business students of economics can complete a ISAP-sponsored semester at the USST Business School in Shanghai. The business school is AACSB-accredited, making it one of the most prestigious business schools in the region. Participating students must have at least 60 ECTS (at least two successfully completed semesters) and good English skills. The courses at USST are chosen in consultation with Prof. Natalia Ribberink and credited towards studies at HAW Hamburg. Further information: HAW Hamburg: ISAP USST In addition, two research stays per semester (approx. 2 weeks, including support for the preparation of master's theses) are funded. BAföG (student financial assistance) for study abroad ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Students who would like to complete a semester abroad can apply for BAföG for study abroad, if they have the prerequisites for BAföG in general. BaföG for study abroad can be combined with various scholarships. The scholarship amounts are deducted from the BAföG contribution. Further information here in German . The application should be submitted to the office responsible six months before the start of your study abroad semester. Please be sure to always note in your application whether you have applied for any scholarships, and which ones. DAAD - HAW.International (worldwide) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ With the HAW.International programme, the German Academic Exchange Service ( DAAD) has started a programme exclusively for HAW Hamburg students in order to support the mobility of this

title: Scholarships and Funding – Studying Abroad
text: Scholarships and Funding – Studying Abroad: target group. Funding is available for study visits and stays abroad as part of a final thesis. The programme provides a full scholarship (monthly scholarship amount, travel subsidy, contribution to insurance), which makes it especially attractive. You can find all the necessary information about application requirements, application deadlines and the scholarship amounts here . You will need to submit your application, with all the necessary documentation, directly to DAAD. Please note: Participants in the Erasmus programme are not eligible for this scholarship. If your application for a HAW.International scholarship from DAAD is successful, you cannot receive a PROMOS scholarship from the HAW Hamburg International Office. Double funding is not permitted. DAAD - One-year study scholarships (worldwide) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Through the one-year scholarship programme, the German Academic Exchange Service ( DAAD ) aims to provide students with the opportunity to gain international experience through a longer study stay abroad. The idea is that students should pursue their own individual study interests and develop in the best possible way, both personally and academically. Funding is available for one-year stays abroad in countries other than the Erasmus+ programme countries . The programme provides a full scholarship (monthly scholarship amount, travel subsidy, contribution to insurance), which makes it especially attractive. You can find all the necessary information about application requirements, application deadlines and the scholarship amounts here . You will need to submit your application, with all the necessary documentation, directly to DAAD. Fulbright scholarships for German students (USA) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The Fulbright Commission awards full and partial scholarships to deepen and supplement studies and research at American universities. The programmes are aimed at German students and graduates who not only want to continue their education in the USA, but also see themselves as ambassadors for their country and want to promote German-American cultural exchange. The following programmes are currently available for German undergraduate and graduate students: Travel scholarships (for study semesters at a US partner university) Study scholarships (for 4-9 months of graduate study) PhD research scholarships (for 4-6 months research) Summer school scholarships (Eastern Europe) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Go East summer and winter schools in Eastern Europe (in German only) (summer school combined with a scholarship from the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD)) HAW.International for travel to congresses and trade fairs (worldwide) ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If you are completing Master's or doctoral studies and would like to actively participate in a trade fair or congress in another country, the German Academic Exchange Service (DAAD) offers funding for this purpose. You can find all the necessary information about application requirements, application deadlines and the scholarship amounts here . You will need to submit your application, with all the necessary documentation, directly to DAAD. Team International / last change 17.03.2026 x

title: Application Info
text: Application Info: HAW-Hamburg: Application BEFORE YOUR STUDIES Study orientation Application (current) Preparing for your studies Your path to the future - apply for one of our Bachelor's or Master's degree programmes! Would you like to deepen your knowledge, discover new perspectives and advance your career in a targeted manner? Our Bachelor's and Master's degree programmes offer you the perfect basis for further developing your professional and personal skills. At our university, you can expect practical teaching content, excellent support and an inspiring learning environment. Whether you have just completed your A-levels or already have a first degree, we will help you realise your professional goals. Find out more about our degree programmes and start your application now! Note on application documents When applying for a Bachelor's or Master's degree programme at HAW Hamburg, no documents must be submitted in paper form. Documents sent to the Student Admisssions and Registration Office in paper form will be destroyed. If documents are required for your application, please upload them as part of the online application. If you can send your application without uploading, no documents are required for your application. General application deadlines ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Winter semester: 1 June–15 July Summer semester: 1 December–15 January The deadlines for some degree courses are different. You can find more information on the specific degree course page . Please also note that the application deadlines are the final deadlines. Applications received after these closing dates will not be considered. Typical application process ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Winter semester Summer semester 15 July 15 January Application deadline Mid-/End of July Mid-/End of January HAW Hamburg sends emails to the applicants admitted in the main application process. If you have been admitted through this process, you can now download your offer of admission from myHAW. If you want to accept the offer, you need to send the signed registration form and all the accompanying documents it lists back to HAW Hamburg by the deadline given! Please don't forget to pay the semester contribution by bank transfer! Mid-August to the beginning of lectures Mid-February to the beginning of lectures HAW Hamburg sends emails to the applicants admitted from the waiting list. If you have been admitted from the waiting list, you can now download your offer of admission from myHAW (see above for login information; for the subsequent steps see the previous box on successful applicants). End of August End of February HAW Hamburg sends emails to the unsuccessful applicants. If you have not been admitted, you can now download the letter informing you that your application was not successful (see above for login information). Please note: Unsuccessful applicants' documents will be destroyed at the end of the semester. Until 30 September Until 31 March An accepted study place can be returned by 30 September (winter semester) or 31 March (summer semester). For a refund of the semester fee (excluding the administration fee of 50 euros). Please send a written confirmation stating your full name and matriculation number to the Student Admissions and Registration Office.

title: Application Info
text: Application Info: Foreign language skills ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Knowledge of English English language skills are required for some degree programmes. If you are unable to provide proof of this when applying, you will not be able to take part in the selection process. You should therefore take care of the required proof in good time. Please refer to the information on the respective degree programme page to find out whether English language skills are a prerequisite for admission to your chosen degree programme. Recognised language certificates for English at level C1 A German Zeugnis der allgemeinen Hochschulreife (colloquially Abitur) or Fachhochschulreife (colloquially Fachabitur), showing a grade of gut (at least 11 points) or better in the subject of English, taken as a Leistungskurs or as an Erweiterungskurs TOEFL (Test of English as a Foreign Language), ITP or iBT IELTS (International English Language Testing System - Academic Training) Cambridge Certificate: - CAE (Cambridge Certificate in Advanced English) - CPE (Certificate of Proficiency in English) TOEIC (Test of English for International Communication) BEC (Business English Certificates) Linguaskill General PTE (Pearson Test of English) Academic telc English A higher education entrance qualification attained at a school in an English-speaking country, as defined by the German Federal Agency for Civic Education , or an English-medium school A formal document/official transcript issued by a higher education institution confirming the applicant has studied successfully for at least two years on a degree course taught through the medium of English or in an English-speaking country, as defined by the German Federal Agency for Civic Education Evidence of professional/work experience, attained after graduation from a degree course, of at least two years’ duration, attained in an English-speaking country, as defined by the German Federal Agency for Civic Education , or in a business or organisation which works in English. Recognised language certificates for English at level B2 A German Zeugnis der allgemeinen Hochschulreife (colloquially Abitur) or Fachhochschulreife (colloquially Fachabitur), showing a grade of ausreichend (4.0, or at least 5 points) or better in the subject of English. Should the applicant’s Zeugnis der allgemeinen Hochschulreife or Fachhochschulreife contain no final grade in the subject of English, the equivalent grade shall be calculated by forming the average (expressed in points) of the partial grades in the subject of English listed in the document for the final two years of schooling. Higher semester ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Below you will find information on applying for a higher semester in degree programmes at HAW Hamburg. If you have any questions, please contact the Student Admission and Registration Office 1. application Applications must be submitted via the HAW Hamburg application portal: myHAW . Online registration and application is only possible via this portal within the regular application deadlines. If you are not yet studying at HAW Hamburg, please create a new application account. If you are already studying here, please log in with your student account and do not create a new application account. 2. general application deadline In the summer semester: 15 January In the winter semester: 15 July By the end of the application deadline (receipt of the application in the applicant portal), the application form must be complete and include all required documents. You will need a placement certificate (see point 6.) from the receiving degree programme confirming that you can be admitted and to which higher semester. 3. documents required for the application For

title: Application Info
text: Application Info: a complete application you will need - University entrance qualification - Certificate of placement - Language certificate if applicable (for applications with a foreign HZB) - If applicable, preliminary examination documentation from uni-assist (for applications with a foreign HZB) You can upload all documents to myHAW during the application phase and submit them later if necessary. No documents need to be sent to the Registrar's Office by post. 4 Admission capacities and selection criteria The number of available study places in degree programmes with restricted admission is calculated for the respective subject-related semester within the standard period of study from the difference between the set number of admissions and the number of enrolled students assigned to this subject-related semester. The university determines the number of available study places. An application is only possible up to the penultimate semester of the standard period of study (example: for a Bachelor's degree programme with a standard period of study of 7 semesters, an application is possible up to a maximum of 6 semesters). When applying, all previous achievements in the desired degree programme must be taken into account. Insofar as there are admission restrictions for the 2nd and further semesters, §§ 13 to 15, § 17 and §19 HAWAZO apply accordingly for the allocation of study places, whereby the selection criteria are regulated in the corresponding admission and selection regulations. Students who were enrolled at the Hamburg University of Applied Sciences (HAW Hamburg) in the past and have been exmatriculated for the purpose of temporarily studying abroad, caring for a child, completing a period of service in accordance with Article 12a of the Basic Law or for a comparable purpose will be given preferential admission to the relevant degree programme in the semester corresponding to the level of performance achieved. 5 Notifications for applicants In order to be informed about the current status of your application, please log in to the application portal with your account. If, for example, your application is incomplete and has therefore been ruled out, you will be provided with more detailed information in the application portal. You will also receive an e-mail notification to the e-mail address you entered in your application as soon as the status of your application changes. 6 Recognition of previous academic achievements in the individual degree programmes As a rule, the chair of the examination board/study counselling of the relevant department at HAW Hamburg is responsible for the recognition of previous academic achievements. Contact details can be found in the respective degree course information . Please use the required form to apply for categorisation. Please also submit the following documents to the responsible department: - current transcript of records / transcript of records - module handbook - Proof of registered achievements, if applicable 7. application with a foreign university entrance qualification Applications from the 2nd semester onwards are also made via uni-assist for applicants with a foreign university entrance qualification. Applicants with a foreign university entrance qualification must also provide proof of German

title: Application Info
text: Application Info: language skills at level C1. Information on this can be found on our website ‘ Language skills ’. Second degree quota ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Why is there no second degree quota in Hamburg? In Hamburg - in contrast to many other federal states - there is no separate quota for second degree applicants. This means that people who have already completed a university degree and would like to take up a further undergraduate degree programme (e.g. a second Bachelor's degree) are considered in the regular selection procedure. Most federal states reserve a certain percentage of study places for second degree applicants (the so-called second degree quota). Hamburg does not do this in order to ensure that all study places are allocated equally. Instead, the same admission rules apply to all applicants, regardless of whether they have already completed a degree programme or not. What does this mean for second degree applicants? Applications are made via the regular procedure, based on A-level grades, waiting time or other selection criteria for the respective degree programme There is no separate allocation of points for second degree programmes, as is often the case in other federal states This can make it more difficult to obtain a study place, particularly in degree programmes with restricted admissions and high demand Important information in the application process During your online application, you will be asked whether you have already completed a degree programme. Behind this question is the second degree quota. However, as this is not relevant in Hamburg, this question will be set to ‘no’ by the application clerk. You can also see this on your control sheet. Do not let this unsettle you, it has no influence on your application! Information for international applicants ................................................................................................................................................................................................ International students are very welcome at Hamburg universities! For a successful application, it is important to note a few special requirements, as the application process for international students differs in some respects from that for applicants with a German educational qualification. To ensure that you receive all the relevant information in a complete and up-to-date form, we have compiled it on a separate page for international applicants. There you will find detailed information on application requirements, deadlines, required documents, and special regulations for international applications. Please be sure to read through this information carefully. All information for international applicants can be found here . Study places are also allocated to international students according to certain selection criteria. The following applies: 10% of the available study places are reserved for applicants from non-EU countries. Applicants from EU countries, on the other hand, are treated in the same way as German applicants in the application process. Explanation of the status information in "myHAW" ................................................................................................................................................................................................ in preparation The status in preparation indicates that an application has been saved in myHAW, but the application has not yet been submitted by the applicant. In this status, the application is not yet being processed further by the university. received The application has been submitted but has not yet

title: Application Info
text: Application Info: been processed by the Registrar's Office. In this status, the application can no longer be changed. If this is desired, the application must be ‘declined’. received withdrawn Applications that are still in ‘received withdrawn’ status, i.e. not yet being processed by HAW Hamburg, can be declined by applicants. The reasons for this are The applicants wish to process the application again. The applicants wish to withdraw from the current award procedure. Applications with the status ‘received withdrawn’ no longer take part in the application process. Applicants can resubmit withdrawn applications within the programme-specific application deadline. valid The application was received on time and has been processed by the university. The application will take part in the award procedure. excluded The application was excluded after examination by HAW Hamburg and will no longer take part in the procedure. This may be the case, for example, if the application was not submitted in the correct form or on time. admission offered Applicants are offered a study place by HAW Hamburg. The offer must now be accepted in the myHAW portal by clicking on the ‘Apply for enrolment’ button to enrol online. It is important to accept the study place within the deadline and to submit the specified documents in paper form to HAW by the deadline. temporarily excluded This status occurs when applicants have to submit additional documents. As a rule, the administrator will explain the reason for this in the information field - for example, ‘Document XY is still missing’. Applicants can find the information field in their myHAW account. The missing documents can only be submitted within the respective application deadline. If this is not done, you will be excluded from the procedure. admission currently impossible Applicants cannot currently be made an offer of admission. The status may change in the course of the admission procedure. admitted You have received a letter of admission. Now click on ‘Apply for enrolment’ in the myHAW portal to enrol online. It is important to accept the study place within the deadline and to submit the specified documents in paper form to the HAW by the deadline. admission withdrawn The applicant has actively returned the admission offer from HAW Hamburg by clicking on ‘admission withdrawn’ in the myHAW account. Applications with the status ‘admission withdrawn’ will no longer take part in the application process. request for enrollment submitted The applicant has decided to accept the study place offered by HAW Hamburg and to apply for enrolment. request for enrollment in progress Your documents have been received and are being checked by the Registrar's Office. Enrolment is currently being processed. There is no guarantee that your documents are complete or correct. request for enrollment accepted The applicant has received a study place at HAW Hamburg and has been enrolled. You will receive your access data for your myHAW student account by e-mail within one week of the status change. The Deutschlandsemesterticket will be made available to you digitally. admission offer expired The applicant has allowed

title: Organising Your Studies
text: Organising Your Studies: HAW-Hamburg: Organising your studies You can find the most important information about organising your studies – including timetables, examination and study regulations, and module handbooks – under your particular degree course . On this page we’ve compiled information about key dates and central services. Overview of degree courses for students Who can I turn to? Lecture and semester dates ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Summer semester 2026: 1 April 2026–30 September 2026 Lecture period: 6 April 2026–1 August 2026 (including the three-week examination period) Winter semester 2026/27: 1 October 2026–31 March 2027 Lecture period: 5 October 2026–13 February 2027 (including the three-week examination period) Lecture-free period: Christmas holidays: 21 December 2026–3 January 2027 Summer semester 2027: 1 April 2027–30 September 2027 Lecture period: 5 April 2027–31 July 2027 (including the three-week examination period) Winter semester 2027/28: 1 October 2027–31 March 2028 Lecture period: 11 October 2027–18 February 2028 (including the three-week examination period) Lecture-free period: Christmas holidays: 20 December 2027–2 January 2028 Summer semester 2028: 1 April 2028–30 September 2028 Lecture period: 3 April 2028–28 July 2028 (including the three-week examination period) Re-registration ................................................................................................................................................................................................ To re-register for the new semester, please pay the semester contribution by bank transfer within the re-registration period. Please do not pay before the start of the re-registration period as the final amount will not be determined before this period begins. The semester contribution for winter semester 2026/27 is €397. Please also transfer the amount even if you are blocked from re-registering. The block can only be lifted if you have paid the semester contribution. Re-registration deadlines: Winter semester: 1 July –15 September Summer semester: 1 January –15 March The semester contribution must be in the HAW Hamburg account by the last day of the re-registration period at the latest! Important: Late re-registration can result in involuntary deregistration from the university. Subsequent re-registration is only possible within the appeals period. Regardless of whether or not deregistration takes place, an additional administrative fee of €15 will be charged for late re-registration. If you are financially unable to pay the semester contribution, you may contact the Students' Union (AStA). In warranted cases it can provide you with a loan. Bank account information Recipient: Hochschule für Angewandte Wissenschaften Hamburg BLZ: 20000000 Bank: Deutsche Bundesbank, Willy-Brandt-Straße 73, 20459 Hamburg IBAN: DE08200000000020101544 BIC: MARKDEF1200 Purpose for winter semester 2026/27: Semester code 20262 and your student ID number (please do not leave a space in between!) last name, first name (please leave a space between the student ID number and your name!), e.g.: 202622567890 Mustermann, Max All the necessary information about the current semester contribution and your payment status is available through the myHAW student and application portal. Health insurance ................................................................................................................................................................................................ All students in Germany are required to have health insurance coverage. Proof of coverage is usually provided upon enrolment . The transfer of information between universities and health insurance companies will now take place digitally. This means you do not need to submit paper documents to us if you change health insurance providers. After 1

title: Organising Your Studies
text: Organising Your Studies: November 2021, please give your new health insurance provider the sender number for HAW Hamburg: H0000531. If you fall behind on your health insurance payments, this information will be provided to us digitally. You will then be blocked from re-registering for the next semester. As soon as you have made the outstanding payments, your health insurance company will inform us and you will be able to re-register. If you are currently enrolled at HAW Hamburg and only change your degree course, you do not need a new certificate of health insurance coverage, unless you switch health insurance providers at the same time you switch degree courses. Student ID card ................................................................................................................................................................................................ From April 1, 2024, chip cards will no longer be issued to new students. The student ID card is available for download in myHAW for all enrolled students. The existing chip cards will remain valid and can be revalidated and then continue to be used as personalized student ID cards. The semester ticket will be made available as a digital ticket via the HVV in the summer semester 2024 as part of the changeover to a discounted Deutschlandticket. Additional information myHAW ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The myHAW application and student portal at HAW Hamburg is used to manage the university's application process, degree course organisation, and examinations. At the current time, students can use their HAW user ID to sign into myHAW and obtain their enrolment, as well as BAföG (student financial aid) certificates. It is also possible to change your address and submit leave and deregistration requests. Semester ticket / Deutschlandticket ................................................................................................................................................................................................ The semester ticket has been changed to a discounted digital Deutschlandticket. Further information is available here. Examinations and transcripts ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Students can access their grades and examination results via myHAW. Leave of absence ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If you are unable to attend at least half of the classes in a semester due to extenuating circumstances, you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you still have to pay the semester contribution. If you apply for leave extending over several consecutive semesters, the administrative fee can be reimbursed. The application for leave must be received by the Student Admissions and Registration Office before the re-registration deadline. You are eligible for a leave of absence if one of the following situations applies: Illness Caring for a child Study abroad Military or alternative civilian service Financial difficulty Leave of absence is not possible in the following cases: Prior to successful completion of first-year academic courses When the timely completion of studies is jeopardised (e.g. in the case of discontinued degree courses) These restrictions do not apply if you are ill, have to care for a relative, are pregnant or need to care for a child. You can download the application form from myHAW (Mein Studium/Studienservice/Beurlaubung) and upload the required documents. Once you have submitted the application, you can no longer reverse it in myHAW. If you have submitted the application in error, please contact the HAW Hamburg Student Admissions and Registration Office

title: Organising Your Studies
text: Organising Your Studies: ( studierendensekretariat (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de ). A processing fee of €15 will be charged for applications received after the deadlines noted above. Bank account information Recipient: HAW Hamburg Bank: Deutsche Bundesbank IBAN: DE08200000000020101544 BIC: MARKDEF1200 Purpose for winter semester 2025/26: Semester code 20252 and your student ID number (please do not leave a space in between!) last name, first name (please leave a space between the student ID number and your name!), also add Beurlaubung e.g.: 202522567890 Mustermann, Max Beurlaubung Changes to personal information / Application forms ................................................................................................................................................................................................ You can find the forms necessary for submitting changes to the Student Admissions and Registration Office on myHAW . Internship semester (please include supporting documents!) You can apply to withdraw via the myHAW portal (Mein Studium/Studienservice/Exmatrikulation). It is no longer necessary to apply in writing. You can also change your address in myHAW yourself. Please note: If you live alone and move within Hamburg, you can change your address with the resident registration office using the eWA (electronic residence registration) online service. To do so, you need a personal ID card with an e-ID function and need to set up a user account. This offer is provided by the City of Hamburg. More information (in German) is available here . Please inform us in writing of the following changes, making sure to include the supporting documents: Name change (please include excerpt from the Familienbuch or for trans people the dgti ID card ) Change in health insurance provider Re-registration for a study semester following an internship semester (request can also be submitted via email) Please send your application and the supporting documents to the Student Admissions and Registration Office (Studierendensekretariat): HAW Hamburg Studierendensekretariat Postfach 10 20 31 D-20014 Hamburg studierendensekretariat (at) haw-hamburg (dot) de If you would like to bring your application to the office at Stiftstraße 69 in person, the mailboxes are located in and in front of the entrance area. Withdrawal and deregistration ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Upon graduation Once you have successfully completed your final Bachelor’s or Master’s examination and the responsible Faculty Service Office has sent a copy of your transcript to the Student Admissions and Registration Office, you will be deregistered from the university. Withdrawal from the university If you have decided to withdraw from HAW Hamburg before completing your studies, please apply to withdraw through the myHAW portal (Mein Studium/Studienservice/Exmatrikulation). Change in degree course If you transfer to a different degree course within HAW Hamburg you do not need to withdraw. Processing time It generally takes four weeks for the withdrawal/deregistration process to be completed. Please take this timeline into account if you want to apply for withdrawal. Once your application has been processed, you will receive confirmation of deregistration and your pension statement. If you require immediate proof of deregistration – for example, because you are changing universities – please state this in your application. Temporarily suspending your studies ................................................................................................................................................................................................ If it is not possible for you to continue studying due to temporary complicating circumstances (e.g. childcare,

title: Organising Your Studies
text: Organising Your Studies: or caring for a family member), you can apply to temporarily suspend your studies. During this time you will not be a HAW Hamburg student, but you are entitled to be re-enrolled in the degree course you have deregistered from until the deadline given. You can obtain more information from the Student Admissions and Registration Office. Exmatriculation ................................................................................................................................................................................................ Exmatriculation from the current semester is possible on a daily basis, at a desired date in the future (not retroactively) or at the end of the respective semester. You can submit the application online in your myHAW portal. The de-registration notice and the pension certificate will be sent to you by post. The processing time can take up to four weeks, please take this into account when submitting your application. If you successfully complete your studies and do not explicitly request exmatriculation, you will be exmatriculated at the end of the semester in which you complete your studies (i.e. March 31st or September 30th). To ensure that you do not suffer any disadvantages, please clarify the following points at the appropriate point before submitting an application for de-registration: • Effects of exmatriculation on financial support (e.g. BAföG office, study foundations, pension funds, etc.) • Effects of de-registration on your health insurance coverage (e.g. health insurance companies) • Effects of exmatriculation on any student employment relationships (e.g. employers) • Effects of exmatriculation on your residence permit if you do not have a German passport (e.g. immigration authorities) • Effects of exmatriculation on the possible need to retake examinations (e.g. study offices, examination offices) Change of course If you change your course of study within HAW Hamburg, you do not have to deregister. myHAW Online services Webredaktion Studierendenzentrum / last change 22.05.2026 x
